title: Beer ball rules — defense, offense, set-screen edge case
html:
Beer ball rules — defense, offense, set-screen edge case

Source: 2023-11-16 Apple Note. The official rule book Yahir wrote for the Corvallis crew's "beer ball" — the precursor sport that becomes Chelympics in 2025.

TL;DR

  • Hit the can, opponents must touch the ball before it returns; one ball-on-table = "down occurs", player stops drinking. Single bounce off can or table allowed before "going down."
  • Defense rules: every defender must touch passes (no double-touch substitutions), clean catches only (no arm-roll/body-bounce), bobble restarts.
  • Offense rules: walk into the lights, 3 drips over a 5-second interval witnessable by opponents, jump-ball after pause, beer-on-ground = automatic new beer (unless opposing team caused it).
  • Open issue noted: "We need a rule about setting 'screens' / getting in way of the ball — says you pussy — if it's not in the rules it'll get abused 🤷‍♂️" — the rule-lawyering instinct on display.
  • This rulebook is the direct ancestor of Beer Ball Tournament event #5 in the Chelympics 2025 score sheet.

Pattern observed

Yahir as rule-author for the boys' games — a recurring role. He documents the format so disputes have a source of truth. Same instinct shows up in Chelympics scoring (5/4/3/2/1 points + penalties for falling/throwing up).

Open questions

  • Did the screen-rule ever get added?
  • Who's the recurring beer-ball squad?
